title: Smoky Quenched French Beef Bouillabaisse
description: A hearty and flavorful beef bouillabaisse dish with a smoky twist, featuring pillowy, puffed bread croutons on top.

ingredients.name: anchovy fillets
ingredients.notes: canned in oil, drained and rinsed

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the diced onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the beef stock concentrate, salt, black pepper, and smoked paprika. Stir to combine.
Add the tomato paste and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the anchovy fillets and cook, stirring occasionally, for 2 minutes.
Add the diced tomatoes and chopped fresh thyme. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes.
Toss the bread cubes with a little olive oil and season with salt. Spread on a baking sheet and toast in the oven until golden brown and crispy, about 10-12 minutes.
To serve, ladle the bouillabaisse into bowls and top with toasted bread croutons and a drizzle of lemon juice.
Garnish with additional fresh thyme, if desired.
This dish is best served immediately, but it can be made ahead and refrigerated for up to 24 hours or frozen for up to 2 months.
